AGNIPATH SCHEME AND AGNIVEER RECRUITMENT 
PART-I 
OVERVIEW 
 
1. History. The Union Cabinet on 14th June 2022 approved an attractive 
recruitment scheme for Indian youth to serve in the Armed Forces called AGNIPATH and the 
youth selected under this scheme will be known as Agniveers. AGNIPATH allows patriotic 
and motivated youth to serve in the Armed Forces for a period of four years. The AGNIPATH 
scheme has been designed to enable a youthful profile of the Armed Forces. 2. About the scheme. "Agnipath Yojana" is a new HR Management scheme for 
the Armed Forces. Candidates inducted through this scheme will be called Agniveers. As part 
of the enrolment process, each ‘Agniveer’ will be required to formally accept all terms and 
conditions of the AGNIPATH Scheme. For personnel below the age of 18 years, the 
enrolment form will need to be signed by the parents/guardians, in accordance with extant 
provisions. Agniveer will be enrolled in the Indian Army under Indian Army Act 1950 for a 
period of four years. Agniveer would form a distinct rank in the Indian Army, different from 
any other existing ranks. Indian Army is not obliged to retain Agniveer beyond the 
engagement period of four years. On completion of four years of service, based on 
organisation’s requirements and policies promulgated by the Indian Army, Agniveer will be 
offered an opportunity to apply for permanent enrolment in the Indian Army. These 
applications will be considered in a centralised manner based on objective criteria including 
performances during their four-year engagement period and upto 25% of each specific batch 
of Agniveer will be enrolled in regular cadre of the Indian Army.

Indian Army is not obliged to retain Agniveer beyond the 
engagement period of four years. On completion of four years of service, based on 
organisation’s requirements and policies promulgated by the Indian Army, Agniveer will be 
offered an opportunity to apply for permanent enrolment in the Indian Army. These 
applications will be considered in a centralised manner based on objective criteria including 
performances during their four-year engagement period and upto 25% of each specific batch 
of Agniveer will be enrolled in regular cadre of the Indian Army. Agniveer will not have any 
right to be selected for further enrolment into the Indian Army. Selection of the 
Agniveer for further enrolment, if any, shall be at the discretion of the Indian Army. 3. Employability. Agniveer enrolled under this entry are liable to be assigned any 
duty in organisational interest, at the discretion of the Indian Army. 4. Training. On being enrolled, Agniveer will be imparted military training based on 
Indian Army requirement. 5. Leave. Grant of leave will be subject to exigencies of Indian Army. The following 
leave may be applicable for the Agniveer during their engagement period: -

in Indian Army, Agniveer will be entitled for medical facility at Service Hospitals as well 
as CSD provisions. 4

their contribution including accrued interest thereon and a matching contribution from 
the Government equal to the accumulated amount of their contribution including

6.3 
Insurance, Death and Disability Compensation. Agniveer 
will 
be 
provided non-contributory Life Insurance cover of Rs 48 lakhs for the duration of their 
engagement period as Agniveer in the Indian Army. Agniveer will not be governed by 
provisions contained in the Pension Regulations/ Rules for Indian Army. 6.4 
Agniveer Skill Certificate. At the end of engagement period, a detailed 
Skill-Set certificate will be provided to the Agniveer highlighting the skills and level of 
competency acquired by the personnel during their engagement period. 6.5 
Personnel enrolled under this scheme will not be eligible for Ex-Servicemen 
Status. 6.6 
Only unmarried candidates will be eligible for enrolment as Agniveer and they 
must undertake not to marry during the defined engagement period of four years. Agniveers who marry during the said period would be released from service. Further, 
only unmarried Agnvieers will be eligible for re-enrolment in regular cadre as Sepoy/ 
equivalent on selection as currently in vogue. Note 1 
Agniveer will not be required to contribute to any 
Provident Fund of the Government. Note 2 
There shall be no entitlement to gratuity and any kind of 
pensionary benefits in case of Agniveer. 5

20. Preparation For Common Entrance Examination (CEE) 
 
20.1 The candidates are advised to check the official syllabus. By knowing the 
detailed syllabus, the candidates will be able to know which subjects, units and topics 
are to be studied for the Common Entrance Examination. The syllabus will consist as 
per qualification required and sample paper of the same is given below as per 
Category. 20.2 Candidates are advised to make a proper timetable to study. They should follow 
the study schedule religiously to avoid the last-minute rush. Studding according to the 
timetable will give the candidates a clear idea of how much time they should spend on 
each topic.

By knowing the 
detailed syllabus, the candidates will be able to know which subjects, units and topics 
are to be studied for the Common Entrance Examination. The syllabus will consist as 
per qualification required and sample paper of the same is given below as per 
Category. 20.2 Candidates are advised to make a proper timetable to study. They should follow 
the study schedule religiously to avoid the last-minute rush. Studding according to the 
timetable will give the candidates a clear idea of how much time they should spend on 
each topic. 20.3 During the preparation process, the candidates should ensure that they clear 
all the basic concepts. This will allow them to have a wholesome idea of the syllabus 
and perform well in the examination. The candidates are advised to learn all the 
formulas in advance. 20.4 Candidates are advised to prepare small notes on each topic wise or difficulty 
wise. They should have a separate notebook for the examination preparation. This 
will allow the candidates to revise anytime anywhere. 20.5 Candidates should try solving papers from authentic sources. Practicing the 
previous year’s question papers will allow the candidates to have an insight into how 
the questions will be prepared and the examination pattern. This will also allow them 
to know their preparation stance. 20.6 Time management will play a crucial role that can either make or break the 
performance of the candidates. Using the sample papers, the candidates should 
practice finishing the exam within the allotted time. 20.7 Revision is an important part of the preparation process. Candidates should 
ensure that they include revision in their daily routine so that they can go over what 
they had earlier studied and have lesser chances of forgetting it. 38

22.1 AVGD Syllabus 
22.1.1 General Knowledge The test will include questions relating to India 
and its neighbouring countries especially pertaining to History, Culture, 
Geography and who's who. In addition, Abbreviations, Sports, Awards and 
prizes, Terminology, Indian Armed Forces, Continents and Sub Continents, 
Inventions and Discoveries, the Constitution of Indian, International 
Organizations, Books and Authors, Knowledge of Important events that have 
happened in India and at world level in the recent years, Current important 
world events, Prominent personalities etc. 22.1.2 General Science. Question of General Science will include topic 
related to Physics, Chemistry and Biology. Based on fundamentals and day to 
day activity (i.e difference between the living and non-living, basis of life- cells, 
protoplasms and tissues, growth and reproduction in plants and animals, 
elementary knowledge of human body and its important organs, common 
epidemics, their causes and prevention etc). 22.1.3 Mathematics. 22.1.3.1 Number Systems. Computation of Whole Number, Decimal 
and Fractions, Relationship between numbers. 22.1.3.2 Fundamental Arithmetical Operations HCF, LCM, Decimal 
fraction, Percentages, Ratio and Proportion, Square roots, Averages, 
Interest (Simple and Compound), Profit and Loss, Discount, Partnership 
Business, Time and distance, Time and work. 22.1.3.3 Basic algebraic problems. 22.1.3.4 Geometry. Familiarity with elementary geometric figures and 
facts. 22.1.3.5 Mensuration Triangle, Quadrilaterals, Regular Polygons, 
Circle etc 40

37. To prepare for the recruitment rallies physical fitness test, focus on a balanced fitness routine 
incorporating 1.6 km running, pull–ups, balancing exercises and potentially ditch jumps and agility 
drills. A structured training plan that includes cardio and strength training, a healthy and balanced 
diet, consistent practice, proper rest and simulating test conditions is crucial for success along with 
staying hydrated. 37.1 Build Stamina through running: - 
37.2 Practice 1.6 km runs daily. 37.3 Incorporate interval training and long distance runs to build endurance. 37.4 Aim to gradually improve your pace to meet the time requirements. 37.5 Enhance Upper Body Strength perform pull-ups daily. 37.6 Start with a manageable number and increase repetitions as your strength grow. 37.7 Include other strength exercise like push-ups and weight training to support overall upper 
body development. 38. Improve flexibility and Agility. 38.1 Incorporate stretching routines and agility drills into your fitness regimen. 38.2 Practice balancing exercises to improve stability and coordination. Yoga can be 
beneficial. 39. Focus on Core Strength. 39.1 Practice sit-ups with correct form to effectively engage your core muscles. 39.2 Add other core exercises such as crunches, leg raises and planks to your routine. 40. Key Physical Fitness Tests. 40.1 1.6 km Run : Assesses cardiovascular endurance. 40.2 Beam/Pull-ups : Tests upper body strength. 40.3 Balancing Test : Evaluates balance and coordination. 40.4 Ditch Jump : Tests explosive power and agility. 74

Q.1 
Can candidates of Rajasthan be enrolled outside Rajasthan in Army? Ans 
No! Candidates of Rajasthan can only be recruited in army rallies conducted 
inside Rajasthan State. If anybody is promising you enrollment in army beyond 
Rajasthan State area, he is definitely trying to cheat you, hence beware. This is 
applicable to other states also for enrolment. Q.2 
Can I take part in rally of other district in the State? Ans No, you can't. You can take part only in your district rally. 88 
 
Q.2 
What are the documents required during recruitment rally? Ans 
Mark sheet, Board certificate, Caste certificate, Domicile certificate and 
Character certificate from Sarpanch in original and photocopy in duplicate duly 
attested by Gazetted Officer/Self attested. Q.3 
If I do not provide my Aadhaar Number will I be rejected? Ans 
No, but you have to update your Aadhaar number to facilitate the recruiting 
organisation for your identification. Q.4 
My elder brother is presently serving in the Army; do I get the benefit of 
physical/bonus marks in Common Entrance Exam? Ans 
Brother of a serving/retired soldier does not get benefit in terms of 
physical/bonus marks. He only gets the benefit of participating in a recruitment rally. Q.6 
I am holding NCC 'B' certificate, what is the benefit in case I produce it in the 
rally? Ans 
In case you have applied for Agniveer GD, Agniveer Clerk / Store Keeper 
Technical, Agniveer Tech and Agniveer Tradesman Category, you will get 10 bonus 
marks in the Common Entrance Exam provided you pass the Common Entrance 
Exam. Q.7 
In what time frame do I need to report to military hospital for review of medical? Ans 
The medical authority at Rally site will intimate you. It is within 07 days

than Click on "PRINT" option and download it.

Character certificate with official stamp duly signed by the Village Sarpanch/ 
Ward Parshad / Headmaster / Principal of a recognized School / College (where the 
candidate is presently studying) will be acceptable. This certificate is valid for six 
months from the date of issue. For Caste certificate a self attested stamp paper is 
required. Q.11 Which certificate is required as a proof of date of birth? Ans 
Education certificate of 10th class from a central / state education board 
recognized for the purpose of employment under central government in addition to 
higher education certificate whenever required. 89 
 
Q.12 There is a minor variation in my name recorded in 'Relationship certificate' 
issue by Record Office of my father and in my 10th mark sheet? What can be 
done to resolve the discrepancy ? Ans 
In case of acceptable / minor variation in the individual's name, father's name 
/ mother's name, surname and date of birth etc as shown on the education certificate 
of the candidate vis-a-vis the documents of the serving/retired soldier/widow/war 
widow, the bonafide of the candidate will need to be established. For the same, an 
affidavit of Rs.10/- non-judicial stamp paper duly countersigned by the Executive 
 
Magistrate Class-1/Notary authorized by Class-1 Magistrate is required to be 
submitted before he is granted the benefit of being of son/brother of serving/retired 
soldier. If any contrary fact comes later to light, the individual will be held accountable 
and the service of the candidate will be terminated in addition to legal action. In case 
the candidate cannot provide the affidavit, no benefit whatsoever shall be granted to 
the individual and he will be processed as a general candidate in open rallies of Army 
Recruiting Office only.
